content.text: The file already exists with a solid first-pass implementation. Let me fix several correctness issues against the spec: wrong initial angle, missing tie-suppression for the leader emphasis, missing per-readout aria-labels, and a `-0.0` display bug.

lines: -    candidateData.reduce((best, c) => (c.score > best.score ? c : best), candidateData[0]).name
+  // A leader is only crowned when its rounded, displayed score strictly
+  // beats every other rounded score. If two or more candidates share the
+  // top rounded value the emphasis is suppressed entirely (no false bold).

content.text: Now let's add the missing aria-labels on score readouts, `aria-hidden` on the decorative SVG text, and use the safe rounding helper for display:

				</text>
			{/each}

content.text: Let me review the final file to confirm everything is consistent.

file.content:

{#each candidateData as c (c.name)} {@const displayScore = roundScore(c.score).toFixed(1)}

content.text: DONE Reviewed and fixed the existing DotAlignment.svelte: corrected the initial probe angle to the spec's 40 degrees (was 90), added tie-suppression so the leader bar/readout is only bolded when its rounded score strictly beats all others (no false leader on ties), added per-readout aria-labels ("c2 score 0.9" format), marked decorative SVG label text aria-hidden, added a safe -0.0 rounding guard for score display, and added preventDefault on pointer drags. Verified the dot-product math produces the exact aligned/orthogonal/anti-aligned/length-cheat behaviors the driven states require, confirmed the single role="slider" (probe) plus role="spinbutton" data-control="length-c1" satisfy the control contract, and confirmed 46px touch targets, mono mono readouts, semantic color tokens, and the reduced-motion block are all in place.

result: DONE Reviewed and fixed the existing DotAlignment.svelte: corrected the initial probe angle to the spec's 40 degrees (was 90), added tie-suppression so the leader bar/readout is only bolded when its rounded score strictly beats all others (no false leader on ties), added per-readout aria-labels ("c2 score 0.9" format), marked decorative SVG label text aria-hidden, added a safe -0.0 rounding guard for score display, and added preventDefault on pointer drags. Verified the dot-product math produces the exact aligned/orthogonal/anti-aligned/length-cheat behaviors the driven states require, confirmed the single role="slider" (probe) plus role="spinbutton" data-control="length-c1" satisfy the control contract, and confirmed 46px touch targets, mono mono readouts, semantic color tokens, and the reduced-motion block are all in place.
